behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because the behavior text is pure function semantics ('evaluates to the correct sum') and can fail inside SUM/range evaluation logic without module handoff. Winner Unit beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess boundary is in scope. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected since no cross-module failure is required by 'A1 through A10' itself; System rejected because behavior does not mention disk, subprocess, or HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory cell context with A1..A10 numeric values, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)` through the formula evaluator/function-dispatch path, assert exact numeric result.

behaviors.description: Cascaded formula reference (`=B1+1`, `B1 = A1*2`) computes correctly.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure mode is at module boundary: reference resolver + dependency evaluation across cells ('referencing another cell that contains a formula'). Integration beats System because this interaction is fully in-process; no real OS/network/subprocess required. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real infra, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stages.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook state with A1 value, B1 formula `=A1*2`, C1 formula `=B1+1`; run recalculation engine and assert C1 reflects cascaded computation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1↔B1) returns circular reference error, no loop.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because cycle detection requires graph-level interaction between at least two cell references ('A1 references B1, B1 references A1'). Integration beats System because detection is algorithmic/in-process and does not require real OS primitives. Rejection-gate: System rejected by behavior text which only specifies dependency logic and error signaling.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 formula `=B1` and B1 formula `=A1`, invoke evaluator/recalc, assert circular-reference error object/code is returned and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this behavior targets function semantics ('VLOOKUP ... returns ... and returns an error') and can be validated as local function evaluation with provided table arguments. Unit beats System because no real infra boundary appears. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected since behavior does not require persistence/recalc orchestration; System rejected due no OS/network/subprocess text.
behaviors.test_description: Call evaluator for `VLOOKUP` with deterministic in-memory table/range args for (1) existing key and (2) missing key; assert returned value and not-found error respectively.

behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because the behavior is conditional-expression logic in one formula ('IF ... IF ...'). Unit beats System because no external infra is involved. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected since no cross-module handoff is required by the stated behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` against representative A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) in-memory and assert each branch output.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this is string-function behavior local to argument handling ('joins ... handles empty cells by skipping'). Unit beats System because no OS/network/subprocess. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected; behavior does not require module boundary failures beyond function implementation.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate CONCATENATE formula with populated and empty input cells plus separator; assert output omits empties instead of introducing extra blank segments.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure appears at namespace/module handoff: parser/reference resolver must map sheet-qualified address to another sheet model ('different sheet ... resolves correctly'). Integration beats System because this is still in-memory workbook wiring, not real OS/network. Integration-brake: no real infra, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Build workbook with Sheet1 formula `=Sheet2!A1+1` and Sheet2 A1 value; run evaluator and assert Sheet1 computed value is correct.

behaviors.description: On referenced cell change, dependents recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because correctness depends on interaction of dependency graph invalidation + scheduler + evaluator ('all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order'). Integration beats System because order correctness can be exposed fully in-process without OS/network/subprocess. Rejection-gate: System rejected since behavior text is about internal recomputation order only.
behaviors.test_description: Create dependency chain/diamond across cells, mutate a source cell, trigger recalc, and assert final values and observed recompute order match topological constraints.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because undo stack/state manager must coordinate with recalculation state across dependent cells ('restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values'). Integration beats System because this state restoration can be validated in-process without real OS boundaries. Integration-brake: no real infra, no AI/ML output, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula affecting dependents, confirm changed outputs, invoke undo, then assert original formula text and all dependent computed values are restored.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and reload with identical formulas/values (including float precision).
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because serialization/deserialization crosses multiple modules. Then System beats Integration because behavior explicitly requires real disk persistence ('Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back'), and mock FS can hide binary format/flush/path/encoding issues. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is deterministic storage fidelity, no AI model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook with representative formulas and floating-point edge values to a real temp-file path using production save API, reload via production load API, and assert formula text/value equality with precision checks.
